summary:
# Summary

The provided source text does not constitute a single academic article, research study, or empirical report suitable for standard scientific summarization. Instead, it appears to be a raw export or a structured list of bibliographic resources related to games-based library services (GBLS). The content is organized into four primary categories: Articles, Books, Lists & Libguides, and Video Resources. Within the "Articles" section, there is a further subdivision for "Analog Games" and "TTRPG & Kids," followed by a list of titles, authors, publication dates, and sometimes sources (e.g., PBS, The Telegraph, Dicebreaker).

The listed articles cover topics such as the benefits of tabletop role-playing games (TTRPGs) during the COVID-19 pandemic, the development of social-emotional skills through gaming, inclusivity in gaming communities, and the use of RPGs for mental health support. Specific titles include "5 Science-Backed Benefits of Playing Tabletop RPGs During (and After) COVID-19" by The Pop Mythologist (May 2020), "Games Build Social-Emotional Skills" by Rachel Pollari (Dec 2022), and a white paper by foundry10 evaluating Game to Grow's TTRPG programming for youth (Feb 2023). Other entries discuss the cultural rise of Dungeons & Dragons, the therapeutic potential of fictional worlds, and practical tips for adjusting RPG mechanics for children.

The "Books" section lists two titles: "Playing Games in the School Library: Developing Game-Based Lessons and Using Gamification Concepts" and "Libraries Got Game: Aligned Learning through Modern Board Games." These titles suggest a focus on pedagogical integration and curriculum alignment within library settings.

The "Lists & Libguides" section references "Meaningful Play (children and teens): General Resources" and "Gaming in Libraries: An Annotated Bibliography," indicating that the source text itself may be part of or derived from a larger annotated bibliography or libguide designed to assist librarians in navigating the landscape of gaming resources.

Finally, the "Video Resources" category is listed but contains no specific entries in the provided text.

Because the source text is a collection of references rather than a standalone study, it lacks an objective research question, methodology, sample population, or empirical findings. It does not present data on participant outcomes, statistical analyses, or qualitative interviews. Consequently, no claims regarding efficacy, causality, or correlation can be extracted from this text itself. The text serves only as a pointer to other works that may contain such information. For the purposes of a Games-Based Library Services literature review, this entry represents a curated resource list rather than primary evidence. It highlights the existence of literature concerning social-emotional learning, mental health benefits, and practical implementation strategies for TTRPGs and board games in library contexts, but it does not provide the substantive content required to evaluate those claims. Any analysis of the benefits or methods described in the cited titles would require accessing the original sources listed, which are not included in this text. Therefore, the summary is limited to describing the structure and scope of the provided bibliographic list.
sourceText:
Articles ● Analog Games ● TTRPG & Kids ○ 5 Science-Backed Benefits of Playing Tabletop RPGs During (and After) COVID-19 by The Pop Mythologist May 2020 ○ Games Build Social-Emotional Skills by Rachel Pollari at Game to Grow, Dec 2022 ○ Benefits of Playing Games by Rebecca Strang, Dec 2017 ○ How a new generation of gamers is pushing for inclusivity beyond the table by PBS Jan 2023 ○ How Dungeons and Dragons Became Cool by The Telegraph, Jul 2022 ○ How role-playing games can give your kid a mental health boost by Liz Krieger, Mar 2021 ○ How Youth Can Build Social and Emotional Skills with Tabletop Role-Playing Games - a while paper by foundry10 evaluating Game to Grow's TTRPG programming for youth, Feb 2023 ○ The Portable Magic Dispenser - Lucas Maxwell interviews Rebecca Strang about running TTRPGs in the library, Jun 2022 ○ RPGs as therapy: How fictional worlds help vulnerable people explore real-life emotions by Tom Ana at Dicebreaker, Sep 2022 ○ Social Rewards in Tabletop Roleplaying Games - by Lauren at Plus One Inspiration, Feb 2022 ○ Tabletop RPGs For Fans Of Stranger Things & Scooby-Doo by Coleman Gailloreto, Sep 2021 ○ Tabletop RPGs for kids and teens: A sortable list of amazing games! by TTRPG Kids, May 2021 ○ Tips and Tricks: How to adjust tabletop RPG mechanics (including D&D) for kids! by TTRPG Kids, Nov 2021 ○ Top Of The Table – Introducing Kids To RPGs by Matt Miller, Jul 2016 ○ Why have games in libraries? - Resource on the GameRT website ● Video Games
Books ● Playing Games in the School Library: Developing Game-Based Lessons and Using Gamification Concepts ● Libraries Got Game: Aligned Learning through Modern Board Games
Lists & Libguides ● Meaningful Play (children and teens): General Resources ● Gaming in Libraries: An Annotated Bibliography
Video Resources
